saturation.criterion: one additional full fresh GPT-5.6 Sol no-skill round revealed no new root-cause failure class

executors.launch_path: codex --ask-for-approval never exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check -m gpt-5.6-sol -c model_reasoning_effort="medium" --sandbox workspace-write -C <eval-run-dir> --output-last-message <eval-run-dir>/last-message.md --json -

checker.launch_path: codex --ask-for-approval never exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check -m gpt-5.6-terra -c model_reasoning_effort="medium" --sandbox workspace-write -C <round-root> --output-last-message <round-root>/terra-check-last-message.md --json -

model_policy.no_prohibited_model_evidence: Terra found no Claude Code, Claude model, Fable, claude, or claude -p evidence in launch metadata, Codex event streams, transcripts, or final outputs.

failure_classes.label: Customer-language evidence is reused as public-ready copy without enough transformation
evidence: Round 1 reused supplied customer phrases including "Every project update turns into vibes" and "tiny Jira sheriff" in public-facing launch/newsletter copy.
Round 2 again placed the same distinctive supplied phrases in public-facing copy.
failure_classes.why_it_matters: Customer wording can inform voice and problem framing, but direct reuse can publish unapproved customer language as brand copy and make the work feel copied rather than brand-authored.
failure_classes.assertion_seed: The output may synthesize themes from supplied customer language, but it must not place distinctive supplied customer phrases into public-facing copy unless the packet explicitly authorizes quotation or direct reuse.
